1000.question: a- or an-.
1000.answer: a- or an- means without or lacking. For example, aphasia means without speech and anoxia means without oxygen.

1001.question: ab-
1001.answer: ab- means away from. For example abduct means to take something away from its normal position.

1002.question: bi- and di-
1002.answer: bi- and di- mean two. For example, bicyle means two wheels.

1003.question: epi-
1003.answer: epi- means upon, above or on top of. For example, epidermis means the top layer of skin.

1004.question: semi-
1004.answer: semi- means half of. For example, semicircle means half a circle. Semi- can also mean partial. For example, semiconscious means partially conscious.

1005.question: brady-
1005.answer: brady- means slow. For example, bradycardia means slow heart rate.

1006.question: dys-
1006.answer: dys- means difficult or painful. For example, dyspnea means to have a hard time breathing

1007.question: hyper-
1007.answer: hyper- means above, or more than normal. For example, hyperglycemia means high blood sugar.

1008.question: hypo-
1008.answer: hypo- means below or less than normal. For example, hypoglycemia means low blood sugar.

1009.question: sub-
1009.answer: sub- means under or below. For example, subcutaneous means under the skin.

1010.question: osteo-
1010.answer: osteo- means bone. For example, osteoporosis means porous bones.

1011.question: homo-
1011.answer: homo- means same. For example, homologous means similar or same.

1012.question: iso-
1012.answer: iso- means same or equal. For example, isometric means equal in measure and can refer to a muscle contraction that creates tension but no change in bone position (like making a tight fist).

1013.question: hetero-
1013.answer: hetero- means different. For example, heterochromia means different colors and is a term used to describe the condition of having two different color of eyes.

1014.question: post-
1014.answer: post- means after. For example, postnatal means after birth.

1015.question: rhin-
1015.answer: rhin- means nose. For example, rhinostomy means surgical procedure to help open the passages of the nose.

1016.question: tachy-
1016.answer: tachy- means fast. For example, tachycardia means fast heart rate.

1017.question: anti-
1017.answer: anti- means against or opposite of. For example, antibiotic means against bacteria.

1018.question: endo-
1018.answer: endo- means within or inside. For example, endoscopy means to use a scope to see inside the body.

1019.question: cardi-
1019.answer: cardi- means heart. For example, cardiomyopathy means heart muscle disease.

1020.question: myo-
1020.answer: myo- means muscle. For example, myocardium means heart muscle.

1021.question: hepat-
1021.answer: hepat- means liver. For example, hepatomegaly means enlarged liver.

1022.question: nephro-
1022.answer: nephro- means kidney. For example, nephritis means inflammation of the kidney.

1023.question: peri-
1023.answer: peri- means around. For example, pericardium means around the heart.

1024.question: trans-
1024.answer: trans- means across or through. For example, transdermal means through the skin.

1025.question: nulli-
1025.answer: nulli- means none or no. For example, nulliparous means having no children.

1026.question: supra-
1026.answer: supra- means above. For example, suprarenal means above the kidney.

1027.question: infra-
1027.answer: infra- means below. For example, infraorbital means below the eye socket.

1028.question: retro-
1028.answer: retro- means behind or backward. For example, retrograde means backward movement.

1029.question: ante-
1029.answer: ante- means before. For example, anteroposterior means front to back.

1030.question: eu-
1030.answer: eu- means good or well. For example, eupnea means good or normal breathing.

1031.question: mal-
1031.answer: mal- means bad or evil. For example, malnutrition means bad nutrition.

1032.question: pseudo-
1032.answer: pseudo- means false or fake. For example, pseudopregnancy means false pregnancy.

1033.question: gastro-
1033.answer: gastro- means stomach. For example, gastritis means inflammation of the stomach.

1034.question: derm-
1034.answer: derm- means skin. For example, dermatitis means inflammation of the skin.

1035.question: auto-
1035.answer: auto- means self. For example, autoimmune means the body attacks itself.

1036.question: gingiv-
1036.answer: gingiv- means gum. For example, gingivitis means inflammation of the gums.
